comment.body:
Oh absolutely did not expect you to give me price levels etc. without knowing portfolio etc. Just trying to learn how I balance weekly read with daily. Your guidance on weekly being context and daily being granular is helpful. A followup question is this one. 

1. There is a possibility of granular intraday negating the weekly read out. For example Tuesday could be a solid bounce after the selloff today so it is better to wait for Tuesday intraday readout before placing any trades. The only other way is having a tight stop loss and exiting if really the granular play does not follow the same pattern as weekly. 
2. In the case of granular intraday matching the weekly read out one could obviously gain from entering a little early. 

Once again only trying to improve my own reading of the guidance and not asking for levels etc.

comment.body:
lets say your asset is at 100 usd
.22-delta put option premium is $2.50
uncertainity is high, market is anxious.

that $2.50 is all extrinsic value.
you need the underlying to go at least to 97.5 usd (-2.5%) in order to get to breakeven. 
bcs all your extrinsic value will decay away.
no such thing with delta-one assets like stocks and futs
comment.raw_body:

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
now, below the center, charm flows are negative, indicating a passive buyback flow by dealers as time goes on.
vanna is positive (simplified), so locally: IV down = delta down = dealers buy delta
comment.raw_body:
now, below the center, charm flows are negative, indicating a passive buyback flow by dealers as time goes on.
vanna is positive (simplified), so locally: IV down = delta down = dealers buy delta

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
above the center, charm flows are positive, so dealers become longer delta over time, so they sell futures,
vanna is negative, so locally if IV goes down, delta goes up, dealers need to sell delta
comment.raw_body:
above the center, charm flows are positive, so dealers become longer delta over time, so they sell futures,
vanna is negative, so locally if IV goes down, delta goes up, dealers need to sell delta
